taskDescription: Drag the correct anatomical terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph about male external genitalia.
textField:
The testis is enclosed by the *tunica albuginea*, which sends inward partitions called *septula testis* dividing it into approximately *250* lobules containing seminiferous tubules. The seminiferous tubules are lined by *Sertoli* cells (supporting) and *spermatogonia* (germ cells).

The cremasteric muscle is derived from the *internal oblique* muscle and functions in *temperature* regulation of the testis by raising and lowering it. The cremaster reflex is mediated by the *genitofemoral* nerve (L1, L2).

Varicocele is almost always *left-sided* because the left testicular vein drains into the *left renal vein* at a right angle, while the right testicular vein drains directly into the *inferior vena cava*. This right-angle drainage creates higher pressure on the left side.

The lymphatic drainage of the testis is to the *para-aortic* nodes (not inguinal), because the testis develops *retroperitoneally* and descends through the inguinal canal guided by the *gubernaculum*. In contrast, the scrotum drains to the *superficial inguinal* nodes.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Review the details you missed.
